title: Missed optimization: fold icmp eq f(a), f(b) to icmp eq a, b where f(x) = xor(x, lshr(ashr(x, 31), 1)) #143630
author: sreedivyakottilingal
description:
Fixes #143630

This patch adds an InstCombine optimization that folds:

icmp eq/ne (xor A, lshr(ashr(A, BW-1), 1)),
(xor B, lshr(ashr(B, BW-1), 1))

into:

icmp eq/ne A, B

The transform is valid because the function:

f(x) = x ^ (lshr(ashr(x, BW-1), 1))

is injective for integer types of any bitwidth.

This canonicalizes comparisons and enables further optimizations.

alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/W2ZFrP

comments.author: llvmorg-github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM PR SUMMARY COMMENT-->

@llvm/pr-subscribers-llvm-transforms

Author: sreedivyakottilingal

<details>
<summary>Changes</summary>

Fixes #<!-- -->143630

This patch adds an InstCombine optimization that folds:

icmp eq/ne (xor A, lshr(ashr(A, BW-1), 1)),
(xor B, lshr(ashr(B, BW-1), 1))

into:

icmp eq/ne A, B

The transform is valid because the function:

f(x) = x ^ (lshr(ashr(x, BW-1), 1))

is injective for integer types of any bitwidth.

This canonicalizes comparisons and enables further optimizations.

---
Full diff: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/198481.diff


2 Files Affected:

- (modified) llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCompares.cpp (+54) 
- (modified) llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/icmp.ll (+151) 


``````````diff
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCompares.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCompares.cpp
index 5481cd4b9ff59..b1260013f3b47 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCompares.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCompares.cpp
@@ -2944,6 +2944,7 @@ Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldICmpDivConstant(ICmpInst &Cmp,
       return replaceInstUsesWith(Cmp, Builder.getTrue());
     if (LoOverflow == -1) // Low bound is less than input range.
       return replaceInstUsesWith(Cmp, Builder.getFalse());
+
     return new ICmpInst(Pred, X, ConstantInt::get(Ty, LoBound));
   case ICmpInst::ICMP_UGT:
   case ICmpInst::ICMP_SGT:
@@ -5975,6 +5976,49 @@ struct OffsetResult {
   }
 };
 
+/// Match the sign-mask xor pattern:
+///   xor X, lshr(ashr(X, BW-1), 1)
+///
+/// This pattern appears in sign-magnitude encoding transformations and is
+/// commonly generated by frontends for certain bit manipulation patterns.
+/// The operation is injective, meaning it preserves equality/inequality:
+///   f(X) = X ^ ((X >> (BW-1)) >> 1)
+///
+/// This allows simplifying:
+///   icmp eq/ne f(X), f(Y)  ->  icmp eq/ne X, Y
+///
+/// Works for scalar integer types and fixed-width vector integer types.
+/// Returns the base value X if V matches the pattern, otherwise nullptr.
+static Value *matchSignMaskXor(Value *V) {
+  Value *X, *AShrVal;
+  const APInt *ShiftAmt1, *ShiftAmt2;
+
+  // Match xor(X, lshr(ashr(X, BW-1), 1)) with commutativity handled by m_c_Xor
+  if (!match(V,
+             m_c_Xor(m_Value(X), m_LShr(m_Value(AShrVal), m_APInt(ShiftAmt2)))))
+    return nullptr;
+
+  // The lshr must be by exactly 1 bit
+  if (*ShiftAmt2 != 1)
+    return nullptr;
+
+  // The inner ashr must use the same X and shift by bitwidth-1 (sign bit)
+  if (!match(AShrVal, m_AShr(m_Specific(X), m_APInt(ShiftAmt1))))
+    return nullptr;
+
+  // Ensure we're working with integer types (scalar or vector)
+  Type *Ty = X->getType();
+  if (!Ty->isIntOrIntVectorTy())
+    return nullptr;
+
+  unsigned BitWidth = Ty->getScalarSizeInBits();
+  // Check for shift by bitwidth-1 (e.g., 31 for i32, 63 for i64, etc.)
+  if (*ShiftAmt1 != BitWidth - 1)
+    return nullptr;
+
+  return X;
+}
+
 /// Offset both sides of an equality icmp to see if we can save some
 /// instructions: icmp eq/ne X, Y -> icmp eq/ne X op Z, Y op Z.
 /// Note: This operation should not introduce poison.
@@ -5983,6 +6027,16 @@ static Instruction *foldICmpEqualityWithOffset(ICmpInst &I,
                                                const SimplifyQuery &SQ) {
   assert(I.isEquality() && "Expected an equality icmp");
   Value *Op0 = I.getOperand(0), *Op1 = I.getOperand(1);
+  if (Op0->getType()->isScalableTy())
+    return nullptr;
+
+  Value *X = matchSignMaskXor(Op0);
+  Value *Y = matchSignMaskXor(Op1);
+
+  if (X && Y && X->getType() == Y->getType()) {
+
+    return new ICmpInst(I.getPredicate(), X, Y);
+  }
   if (!Op0->getType()->isIntOrIntVectorTy())
     return nullptr;
 
diff --git a/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/icmp.ll b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/icmp.ll
index 61ae46ba08d2d..edfe5b4aaeea5 100644
--- a/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/icmp.ll
+++ b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/icmp.ll
@@ -6238,3 +6238,154 @@ entry:
   %cmp = icmp ult i8 %p0, %p1
   ret i1 %cmp
 }
+
+;  positive-test:Basic case: both sides match the sign-mask xor pattern on i32.
+; f(x) == f(y) --> x == y
+define i1 @signmask_xor_eq_i32(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define i1 @signmask_xor_eq_i32(
+; CHECK-SAME: i32 [[X:%.*]], i32 [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp eq i32 [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[CMP]]
+;
+  %asr_x = ashr i32 %x, 31
+  %lsr_x = lshr i32 %asr_x, 1
+  %xor_x = xor i32 %x, %lsr_x
+  %asr_y = ashr i32 %y, 31
+  %lsr_y = lshr i32 %asr_y, 1
+  %xor_y = xor i32 %y, %lsr_y
+  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor_x, %xor_y
+  ret i1 %cmp
+}
+
+;  positive-test:Same as above but with ne predicate.
+; f(x) != f(y) --> x != y
+define i1 @signmask_xor_ne_i32(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define i1 @signmask_xor_ne_i32(
+; CHECK-SAME: i32 [[X:%.*]], i32 [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp ne i32 [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[CMP]]
+;
+  %asr_x = ashr i32 %x, 31
+  %lsr_x = lshr i32 %asr_x, 1
+  %xor_x = xor i32 %x, %lsr_x
+  %asr_y = ashr i32 %y, 31
+  %lsr_y = lshr i32 %asr_y, 1
+  %xor_y = xor i32 %y, %lsr_y
+  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %xor_x, %xor_y
+  ret i1 %cmp
+}
+
+;  positive-test:i64 variant: shift amounts are 63 and 1 for 64-bit sign-mask xor.
+define i1 @signmask_xor_eq_i64(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define i1 @signmask_xor_eq_i64(
+; CHECK-SAME: i64 [[X:%.*]], i64 [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp eq i64 [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[CMP]]
+;
+  %asr_x = ashr i64 %x, 63
+  %lsr_x = lshr i64 %asr_x, 1
+  %xor_x = xor i64 %x, %lsr_x
+  %asr_y = ashr i64 %y, 63
+  %lsr_y = lshr i64 %asr_y, 1
+  %xor_y = xor i64 %y, %lsr_y
+  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %xor_x, %xor_y
+  ret i1 %cmp
+}
+
+; positive-test: i64 ne predicate.
+define i1 @signmask_xor_ne_i64(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define i1 @signmask_xor_ne_i64(
+; CHECK-SAME: i64 [[X:%.*]], i64 [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp ne i64 [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[CMP]]
+;
+  %asr_x = ashr i64 %x, 63
+  %lsr_x = lshr i64 %asr_x, 1
+  %xor_x = xor i64 %x, %lsr_x
+  %asr_y = ashr i64 %y, 63
+  %lsr_y = lshr i64 %asr_y, 1
+  %xor_y = xor i64 %y, %lsr_y
+  %cmp = icmp ne i64 %xor_x, %xor_y
+  ret i1 %cmp
+}
+
+; positive-test: xor is commutative: lshr(...) xor x is the same pattern as x xor lshr(...).
+; matchSignMaskXor uses m_c_Xor so both orderings should fold.
+define i1 @signmask_xor_commuted_i32(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define i1 @signmask_xor_commuted_i32(
+; CHECK-SAME: i32 [[X:%.*]], i32 [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp eq i32 [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[CMP]]
+;
+  %asr_x = ashr i32 %x, 31
+  %lsr_x = lshr i32 %asr_x, 1
+  %xor_x = xor i32 %lsr_x, %x
+  %asr_y = ashr i32 %y, 31
+  %lsr_y = lshr i32 %asr_y, 1
+  %xor_y = xor i32 %lsr_y, %y
+  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor_x, %xor_y
+  ret i1 %cmp
+}
+
+; positive-test: Vector splat: m_APInt matches splat constants so the fold applies
+; element-wise to the whole vector at once.
+define <4 x i1> @signmask_xor_eq_vec_i32(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define <4 x i1> @signmask_xor_eq_vec_i32(
+; CHECK-SAME: <4 x i32> [[X:%.*]], <4 x i32> [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp eq <4 x i32> [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <4 x i1> [[CMP]]
+;
+  %asr_x = ashr <4 x i32> %x, <i32 31, i32 31, i32 31, i32 31>
+  %lsr_x = lshr <4 x i32> %asr_x, <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>
+  %xor_x = xor <4 x i32> %x, %lsr_x
+  %asr_y = ashr <4 x i32> %y, <i32 31, i32 31, i32 31, i32 31>
+  %lsr_y = lshr <4 x i32> %asr_y, <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>
+  %xor_y = xor <4 x i32> %y, %lsr_y
+  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %xor_x, %xor_y
+  ret <4 x i1> %cmp
+}
+
+; positive-test: Vector ne predicate.
+define <4 x i1> @signmask_xor_ne_vec_i32(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define <4 x i1> @signmask_xor_ne_vec_i32(
+; CHECK-SAME: <4 x i32> [[X:%.*]], <4 x i32> [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp ne <4 x i32> [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <4 x i1> [[CMP]]
+;
+  %asr_x = ashr <4 x i32> %x, <i32 31, i32 31, i32 31, i32 31>
+  %lsr_x = lshr <4 x i32> %asr_x, <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>
+  %xor_x = xor <4 x i32> %x, %lsr_x
+  %asr_y = ashr <4 x i32> %y, <i32 31, i32 31, i32 31, i32 31>
+  %lsr_y = lshr <4 x i32> %asr_y, <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>
+  %xor_y = xor <4 x i32> %y, %lsr_y
+  %cmp = icmp ne <4 x i32> %xor_x, %xor_y
+  ret <4 x i1> %cmp
+}
+
+; positive-test: i4 multi-use variant: icmp folds, but xor computations
+; survive because they have other uses.
+define i4 @signmask_xor_eq_multiuse_i4(i4 %x, i4 %y) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define i4 @signmask_xor_eq_multiuse_i4(
+; CHECK-SAME: i4 [[X:%.*]], i4 [[Y:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = icmp eq i4 [[X]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP2:%.*]] = zext i1 [[TMP1]] to i4
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP3:%.*]] = xor i4 [[Y]], [[X]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP4:%.*]] = ashr i4 [[TMP3]], 3
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP5:%.*]] = lshr i4 [[TMP4]], 1
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP6:%.*]] = xor i4 [[TMP5]], [[TMP2]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP7:%.*]] = xor i4 [[TMP6]], [[Y]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP8:%.*]] = xor i4 [[TMP7]], [[X]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i4 [[TMP8]]
+;
+  %1 = ashr i4 %x, 3
+  %2 = lshr i4 %1, 1
+  %3 = xor i4 %2, %x
+  %4 = ashr i4 %y, 3
+  %5 = lshr i4 %4, 1
+  %6 = xor i4 %5, %y
+  %7 = icmp eq i4 %3, %6
+  %8 = zext i1 %7 to i4
+  %9 = xor i4 %3, %6
+  %10 = xor i4 %9, %8
+  ret i4 %10
+}

``````````

</details>
